summary: All 18 verification checks passed. CI workflow file is valid YAML with correct triggers, steps, and ordering. package.json scripts replaced (clean removed, build+check real). Tests: 57 pass, 0 fail. Build: exit 0. No dist/ created. Working tree clean. 1 unchecked risk: actual GitHub Actions CI run (requires push to GitHub).

passedChecks.result: YAML valid (exit 0). Note: 'on' key is quoted as '"on"' to prevent PyYAML boolean coercion — GitHub Actions best practice (actionlint recommends this).

passedChecks.command: python3 -c "import yaml; y=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); assert 'push' in y['on'] and 'main' in y['on']['push']['branches']; assert 'pull_request' in y['on'] and 'main' in y['on']['pull_request']['branches']"
passedChecks.expected: CI triggers on push to main and pull_request to main
Build step runs 'bun build index.ts --target bun'
Checkout step appears before Setup Bun step

passedChecks.command: python3 -c "import json; s = json.load(open('package.json'))['scripts']; assert 'clean' not in s; assert s['build'] == 'bun build index.ts --target bun --external @oh-my-pi/pi-ai --external @oh-my-pi/pi-coding-agent'; assert s['check'] == 'bun test test/'"
passedChecks.expected: clean removed; build is real build command; check is bun test test/

passedChecks.result: 57 pass, 0 fail, 179 expect() calls, Ran 57 tests across 5 files. [266ms]. Exit 0.

passedChecks.expected: No dist/ directory created by build (build writes to stdout)
passedChecks.result: ls: cannot access 'dist/': No such file or directory (exit 2 — expected)

passedChecks.expected: Commit 'chore: add CI workflow + fix package.json scripts' present
passedChecks.result: 58d114f chore: add CI workflow + fix package.json scripts

passedChecks.expected: Changed files match plan blast radius: .github/workflows/ci.yml + package.json (+ bead artifacts)

uncheckedRisks: Risk: 'oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 action changes or breaks' — cannot verify actual GitHub Actions execution without pushing to GitHub and observing a live CI run. The action is pinned to major version @v2 following semver, but the actual run hasn't been observed. Reason: requires push to GitHub remote, which happens at /pr.

Risk: 'CI is slow, discouraging developers' — actual CI wall-clock time (including runner setup) cannot be measured locally. Local test+build is <1s, but GitHub Actions adds ~10-15s for checkout+setup. Reason: requires live CI run.

Risk: 'Tests fail in CI due to environment differences (OS, Bun version)' — tests were verified in a clean local environment simulating CI (during /create investigation), but the actual ubuntu-latest runner with bun-version:latest may differ. Reason: requires live CI run.

Risk: 'GitHub Actions YAML syntax error' — YAML is valid per PyYAML, but GitHub Actions uses its own YAML parser. The quoted "on" key is compatible with both. Reason: requires live CI run to confirm GitHub Actions accepts the workflow.

Risk: 'Build step produces unexpected output in CI' — build output is verified locally as going to stdout (19,623 bytes, no files). CI runner behavior should be identical but isn't directly observed. Reason: requires live CI run.

Risk: '--external flag behavior changes in future Bun versions' — verified with bun 1.3.14 locally. CI uses bun-version:latest which may differ. Reason: requires live CI run with whatever 'latest' resolves to at run time.

Risk: 'bun build --target bun --external flags don't work as expected in CI' — verified locally in clean env simulation (exit 0, 19,623 bytes) during /create. The actual GitHub Actions environment may differ. Reason: requires live CI run.

artifacts.purpose: GitHub Actions CI workflow — runs bun test + bun build on push/PR to main
Updated scripts: real build and check commands replacing echo stubs

followUps: Verify first live CI run after PR merge — confirms GitHub Actions accepts the workflow and all steps pass on ubuntu-latest. This is the unchecked risk that requires push to GitHub.
Fix pre-existing type errors at index.ts:376,379 (Model<Api> | undefined not assignable to Model<Api>) — separate bead, enables adding tsc --noEmit to CI.
Consider branch protection rules requiring CI to pass before merge — GitHub repo setting, natural follow-up after CI is live.
